summary: A superb sound‑board capture of a high‑energy December ’71 show highlighted by a monster The Other One → Sitting on Top of the World sandwich, blistering Good Lovin’, and an unforgettable Brokedown Palace.
review: The performance is widely praised for its soaring second set – the seamless Other One → Sittin’ on Top of the World transition, massive Good Lovin’, Truckin' and a fiery China Cat jam make this one of the band’s December‑71 peaks. The soundboard source delivers clear, well‑balanced audio (A‑ level) with only occasional hiss or slight tuning quirks that don’t detract from the overall listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most 5‑star, rave about both musical performance and sound quality; only minor technical complaints. The show’s energy and standout moments dominate, justifying an exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced (often described as A‑), piano and keyboards sound sweet. A few reviewers note slight hiss/distance and one out‑of‑tune cassette issue, but overall the mix is listenable and captures the band’s dynamics.

standout_songs: Sitting on Top of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is the highlight – it builds from Truckin' through a flawless Other One → Sittin’ on Top of the World sandwich to a soaring finish with Not Fade Away and China Cat riffs. The first set is solid but less spectacular.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for perfect guitar tone, leading the band into new material and delivering powerful solos throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as providing perfect bass support, anchoring the high‑energy jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for emerging vocal presence (backing Pig on NFA) and strong rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s early piano/organ tone is highlighted as “sweet” and “player‑piano sound,” showing his quick integration into the group.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is called “perfect support” and the post‑drums transition on The Other One is hailed as one of the best ever.
